turns.content: I think if I just use a HashMap to store the values and a LinkedList for the keys, adding items will automatically keep the least recently used one at the front when I call push_front. Why isn't this working correctly in my implementation?

The issue is that HashMap does not track access order; it only maps key to value via impl Hash and PartialEq traits, so inserting or looking up an item does not move its position on your LinkedList. You need to manually update the position of every accessed entry using the LinkedList's splice method or remove_and_push_front methods, otherwise your cache will grow unbounded instead of evicting old entries when capacity is reached.

That makes sense about HashMap ignoring access order, but I'm still confused why my manual eviction logic isn't triggering even though I check size. It seems like the cache just keeps growing forever instead of dropping items that haven't been used recently.

The real problem is likely in how you manage the LinkedList; a proper LRU needs to track usage history explicitly, not rely on HashMap insertion order. When an item is accessed, you must move its node to the front using insert_before or splice methods so it becomes most recently used, and when capacity exceeds limit, remove from the back using pop_back instead of arbitrary removals. This ensures items truly unused for longer stay at the tail where they get evicted first.
